---
# Discover a dataset's fields before authoring (never guess a field name)

Guessing a field name is the #1 first-shot failure. Discover the real schema first. The universal, license-independent method is to sample one row and read its columns: `dataset = X | fields * | limit 1` returns every native column present (xdr_data has ~1158). For a sparse/quiet dataset sample more rows and union the keys (a field absent from every sampled row won't show). Guardian's `xsiam.datamodel_describe(dataset="X")` does this for you — it tries the XDM `datamodel` stage and falls back to sampling, returning `{method, field_count, fields}`. NOTE: the standalone `| datamodel` stage is NOT available on every tenant (it can return `bad query syntax`), which is exactly why the sampling fallback exists.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| fields *
| limit 1
```

_Field-discovery behaviour live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant: `| fields * | limit 1` returned ~1158 native xdr_data columns; `get_datasets` returned 620 datasets; the `| datamodel` stage returned `bad query syntax` (hence the sampling fallback). Adapt the second query's fields to your cloud dataset's real schema (discover them first)._
